subUnits.title: 6.1 Prime Numbers & Prime Factorization
notes: A prime number has exactly two factors: 1 and itself (e.g., 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13).
Composite numbers have more than two factors. 1 is neither prime nor composite.
Prime factorization breaks a number into a product of prime numbers using factor trees or repeated division.

quiz.q: Which of these is a prime number?
Why is 1 not a prime number?
quiz.explanation: Prime numbers must have exactly two distinct factors. 1 only has one.

subUnits.title: 6.2 HCF & LCM
notes: HCF (Highest Common Factor) is the largest number that divides two or more numbers exactly.
LCM (Lowest Common Multiple) is the smallest number that is a multiple of two or more numbers.
Find HCF by listing factors or using prime factorization (multiply common primes).
Find LCM by listing multiples or using prime factorization (multiply all primes at highest power).

quiz.explanation: Factors of 12: 1,2,3,4,6,12. Factors of 18: 1,2,3,6,9,18. Highest common = 6.
Multiples of 4: 4,8,12... Multiples of 6: 6,12... Lowest common = 12.

quiz.q: Which method finds HCF using prime factors?
quiz.explanation: HCF = product of the lowest power of common prime factors.
